summary: A polished, vertical social-media-native montage of Secretary Noem's diplomatic trip to Mexico, scored to music with no spoken transcript. It cuts between Noem deplaning from a DHS jet, dramatic B-roll of the border wall, a side-by-side with President Sheinbaum, and a cinematic slow-walk shot through a government building, with kinetic on-screen captions framing the trip as 'peace through strength.' The cinematic editing, music bed, statesman-glamour styling and slogan captions make it produced spectacle aimed at projecting strength rather than a raw event recording.

script.scene: Noem descends the airstairs of a Department of Homeland Security jet (DHS seal visible on fuselage) onto the tarmac; a suited security detail leads, officials in sunglasses wait to greet her.

Cinematic low-angle B-roll of the rust-colored steel border wall stretching into the desert under blue sky; caption: 'While Mexico has deployed 10,000 National Guard to help secure our Southern border, there is still more work to be done.'

Noem (light blue suit) stands smiling beside Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum (maroon) in an ornate wood-paneled hall; caption: 'Secretary Noem thanked President Sheinbaum for accepting deportation flights.'
Slow-motion 'power walk' tracking shot of Noem striding through a stone colonnade flanked by aides and officials; caption: '...e showing what peace through strength really looks like.'
Closing animated DHS eagle seal on a textured deep-blue background.

notable_text: While Mexico has deployed 10,000 National Guard to help secure our Southern border, there is still more work to be done.
Secretary Noem thanked President Sheinbaum for accepting deportation flights.
...showing what peace through strength really looks like.
